metadata.name: Longhorn
metadata.description: Helm chart for Longhorn, a distributed block storage system for Kubernetes with built-in backup, snapshots, and disaster recovery.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Helm chart for Longhorn, a distributed block storage system for Kubernetes with built-in backup, snapshots, and disaster recovery.

## Prerequisites
- Review Longhorn's environment checks and prepare the required disks, mount points, and host services on every storage node before install.
- If you enable the default backup target, provision that object store or NFS target first and confirm credentials are available to the cluster.
- Make sure the nodes that will host Longhorn have enough free capacity and stable network connectivity.

## Deploy
- Render `values.yaml`, then install or update the release with `helm upgrade --install`.
- Wait until the Longhorn manager and UI pods are ready and the nodes appear healthy in the UI.

## Post-Install
- Verify that every intended node and disk is schedulable before creating production volumes.
- If backups are enabled, run one backup and one restore test before relying on the target for disaster recovery.
- Take a snapshot and attach a test volume so you confirm the CSI path works end to end.

## References
- [Longhorn](https://longhorn.io/)
- [Documentation](https://longhorn.io/docs/)
- [Helm Chart](https://charts.longhorn.io)

items.name: backup_target
items.title: Backup Target
items.description: Backup target URL (e.g., s3://bucket or nfs://server/path)
